title: Define approval enforcement and approval-tracking surface
summary: Turn approvalBoundary from a routing hint into a clearer runtime contract with explicit approval recording and operator-visible enforcement points.
humanSummary: Proposal to design a real approval-enforcement surface for eclawlution so risky changes are not only labeled, but also routed and tracked more explicitly.
rationale: Current helpers classify risk and approval boundaries, but they do not enforce them at runtime.
Daily repo review found a trust gap between the repo's clear security language and the still-thin runtime enforcement surface.
A real approval contract would reduce over-promising and make risky autonomy easier to audit.
risks: Could imply OpenClaw runtime hooks or approval plumbing that do not yet exist.
Could create docs and implementation drift if approval recording lands only partially.
A rushed design could add operator friction or surprise without actually improving safety.
rollbackPlan: revert docs and runtime-surface changes back to advisory approval labels only
re-run local checks and examples after reverting
validationChecks: Keep the first step additive and local-only, without auth, secrets, or live restart behavior.
Ensure README, plugin contract, security model, and proposal lifecycle all describe the same approval surface.
Prefer operator-visible approval state over hidden automation.
nextActions: Define the minimum approval lifecycle states and who sets them.
Decide whether enforcement lives in plugin hooks, review artifacts, or orchestrator-side workflow wiring first.
Review the design with a maintainer before implementing runtime behavior changes.
